claim: Independent verification confirms YOLO demand evidence dispatch implements provider-specific configuration isolation and boundary enforcement to prevent drift between Claude, Codex, and custom providers. All 16 evidence records from explorer are accurate with correct file paths and line numbers.

evidence.summary: executionConfig function independently verified to handle provider-specific normalization; lines 446-448 clear Claude model from Codex provider, lines 449-461 normalize Claude tool profile to semantic modes
evidence.why: This cross-check confirms the core provider isolation logic is implemented as claimed

evidence.summary: Confirmed Codex provider clears ai.model if it contains claude to prevent cross-provider leakage
evidence.why: This is the specific anti-leakage mechanism the explorer identified

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot and diffBoundarySnapshots independently verified as the boundary enforcement core
evidence.why: This cross-check confirms the boundary implementation exists and matches explorer description

evidence.summary: gitFiles function confirmed to use git ls-files -co --exclude-standard -z for efficient file tracking
evidence.why: Cross-check verifies the git-based tracking implementation matches claim

evidence.summary: walkFiles fallback confirmed to handle non-git projects with directory walking
evidence.why: Cross-check confirms the fallback mechanism works as described

evidence.summary: Runtime dispatch confirmed to call buildBoundarySnapshot before (line 515) and after (line 565) agent execution
evidence.why: This cross-check verifies the actual runtime enforcement of boundaries

evidence.summary: Boundary violations confirmed to become blockers that prevent PRD readiness via boundaryBlockers mapping
evidence.why: Cross-check confirms that boundary violations translate to readiness blockers

evidence.summary: settingsValue function independently verified as recent addition to resolve JSON vs file path settings
evidence.why: Cross-check confirms the recent change the explorer identified from git status

evidence.summary: buildProviderInvocation independently verified to handle provider-specific CLI construction; Codex (lines 43-60), custom (lines 63-75), Claude (lines 77-108)
evidence.why: Cross-check confirms provider separation exists at invocation level

evidence.summary: PROVIDER_ALIASES mapping confirmed: anthropic/claude->claude, openai/codex->codex, shell/local->custom
evidence.why: Cross-check verifies the provider name normalization logic

evidence.summary: normalizeAgentProvider function confirmed to map aliases and handle auto detection
evidence.why: Cross-check confirms the provider normalization implementation

evidence.summary: Codex provider isolation test independently verified to check model clearing and sandbox defaults
evidence.why: Cross-check confirms test coverage for provider isolation as claimed

evidence.summary: Boundary enforcement test independently verified to detect project mutations even when agent results pass
evidence.why: Cross-check confirms test proves boundary blocks violations

evidence.summary: Git-based boundary test independently verified to detect untracked mutations via git ls-files
evidence.why: Cross-check confirms git-based boundary tracking test exists

evidence.summary: Non-git fallback test independently verified to detect modified files and ignore artifact directory
evidence.why: Cross-check confirms fallback boundary tracking test coverage

evidence.summary: Doctrine independently verified to specify Claude dispatch does not use tool restriction flags; real safety comes from prompt contract and boundary enforcement
evidence.why: Cross-check confirms the product requirement driving the implementation

evidence.summary: Recent changes to provider-adapter.ts independently confirmed as 22 line additions including settingsValue function and additional Claude flags
evidence.why: Cross-check verifies active development on provider isolation as claimed

evidence.summary: Commit message independently verified as 'fix: make demand PRDs pass executable gates'
evidence.why: Cross-check confirms the demand system is being actively refined

risks: If provider isolation logic has a bug, cross-provider model or config leakage could cause silent dispatch failures
If boundary snapshot misses a file type (e.g., symlinks, special files), mutations could go undetected
If git ls-files fails silently, boundary enforcement could fall back to incomplete directory walking
missing: No evidence of actual stress testing with real Claude -p agents in production demand evidence mode
No evidence of integration tests running full dispatch against real provider binaries
No evidence of boundary enforcement performance testing on large repos

result.notes: All 16 evidence records from explorer independently verified with accurate file paths, line numbers, and summaries. Provider isolation and boundary enforcement are comprehensively implemented with strong test coverage. No conflicts or contradictions found. The implementation is sound but would benefit from real-world stress testing evidence to validate production readiness.
